2603.23251.title: Is AI Catching Up to Human Expression? Exploring Emotion, Personality, Authorship, and Linguistic Style in English and Arabic with Six Large Language Models
2603.23251.paper_summary: The advancing fluency of LLMs raises important questions about their ability to emulate complex human traits, including emotional expression and personality, across diverse linguistic and cultural contexts. This study investigates whether LLMs can convincingly mimic emotional nuance in English and personality markers in Arabic, a critical under-resourced language with unique linguistic and cultural characteristics. We conduct two tasks across six models:Jais, Mistral, LLaMA, GPT-4o, Gemini, and DeepSeek. First, we evaluate whether machine classifiers can reliably distinguish between human-authored and AI-generated texts. Second, we assess the extent to which LLM-generated texts exhibit emotional or personality traits comparable to those of humans. Our results demonstrate that AI-generated texts are distinguishable from human-authored ones (F1>0.95), though classification performance deteriorates on paraphrased samples, indicating a reliance on superficial stylistic cues. Emotion and personality classification experiments reveal significant generalization gaps: classifiers trained on human data perform poorly on AI-generated texts and vice versa, suggesting LLMs encode affective signals differently from humans. Importantly, augmenting training with AI-generated data enhances performance in the Arabic personality classification task, highlighting the potential of synthetic data to address challenges in under-resourced languages. Model-specific analyses show that GPT-4o and Gemini exhibit superior affective coherence. Linguistic and psycholinguistic analyses reveal measurable divergences in tone, authenticity, and textual complexity between human and AI texts. These findings have implications for affective computing, authorship attribution, and responsible AI deployment, particularly within underresourced language contexts where generative AI detection and alignment pose unique challenges.
2603.23251.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型的流利度提升引發了關於它們在多元語言和文化背景下模擬複雜人類特徵（包括情感表達和個性）的能力的重要問題。這項研究探討了大型語言模型是否能夠在英語中令人信服地模仿情感細微差別，以及在阿拉伯語中模仿個性標記，阿拉伯語是一種資源匱乏的關鍵語言，具有獨特的語言和文化特徵。我們對六個模型進行了兩項任務：Jais、Mistral、LLaMA、GPT-4o、Gemini 和 DeepSeek。首先，我們評估機器分類器是否能可靠地區分人類創作的文本和 AI 生成的文本。其次，我們評估大型語言模型生成的文本在多大程度上展現出與人類相當的情感或個性特徵。我們的結果顯示，AI 生成的文本與人類創作的文本是可以區分的（F1>0.95），儘管在改寫樣本上的分類性能下降，這表明對表面風格線索的依賴。情感和個性分類實驗揭示了顯著的泛化差距：在 AI 生成的文本上，基於人類數據訓練的分類器表現不佳，反之亦然，這表明大型語言模型以不同於人類的方式編碼情感信號。重要的是，通過 AI 生成的數據增強訓練，提高了阿拉伯語個性分類任務的性能，突顯了合成數據在解決資源匱乏語言挑戰中的潛力。模型特定分析顯示，GPT-4o 和 Gemini 在情感一致性方面表現優越。語言學和心理語言學分析揭示了人類文本和 AI 文本在語調、真實性和文本複雜性方面的可測量差異。這些發現對情感計算、作者歸屬和負責任的 AI 部署具有重要意義，特別是在資源匱乏的語言背景中，生成性 AI 的檢測和對齊面臨獨特挑戰。
2603.23251.author: Nasser A Alsadhan et.al.
